metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: No `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` items found.
findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that coach_moves.py also needs stdlib `import json` and `from pathlib import Path` (used by `load_move_registry`); update the 'imports ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel' claim to 'imports rebar.llm.review_kernel plus stdlib json and pathlib'.
evidence: passes.py:36-39: 'import json' and 'from pathlib import Path' are present
passes.py:826-828: load_move_registry uses 'path = Path(repo_root) / ".rebar" / "plan_review_moves.json"' and 'json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))'
The plan then correctly says these same imports become 'now-unused' in passes.py after the move — confirming they travel with load_move_registry to coach_moves.py
An implementer reading 'imports ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel' may omit stdlib imports, producing a broken coach_moves.py
findings.finding: The plan asserts coach_moves.py will import 'ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel', but load_move_registry (the function being moved) also uses stdlib json and pathlib.Path — both currently imported in passes.py precisely because load_move_registry needs them.
findings.location: MOVE section: 'The new leaf imports ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel (for validate_move_registry + DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD) — no back-reference to passes.py, so no cycle.'
scenarios: Implementer creates coach_moves.py with only 'from rebar.llm.review_kernel import ...' and no stdlib imports, causing NameError on json/Path at runtime when load_move_registry is called with a non-None repo_root.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the import description to: 'The new leaf imports rebar.llm.review_kernel (for validate_move_registry + DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD) plus stdlib json and pathlib — no back-reference to passes.py, so no cycle.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for the architecture.md deletion, e.g. `grep -c 'passes.py' docs/architecture.md` returning 0 (or a targeted grep for the offenders-table row).
evidence: No grep or diff command is listed for docs/architecture.md
test_module_size_contract.py covers the allowlist/ceilings files but not the markdown prose table
findings.finding: AC #3 requires the remedy row to be deleted from docs/architecture.md's offenders table, but no proving command is specified for this documentation change — it can be forgotten without failing any listed test.
findings.location: Acceptance criteria, bullet 3: 'its remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md offenders table'
scenarios: The developer removes the allowlist entries but forgets the docs row; all pytest and lint ACs pass; the stale architecture doc ships.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #3: `grep -qv 'passes\.py' docs/architecture.md || (echo 'stale offenders row found' && exit 1)` passes.

evidence: verify command `wc -l` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
verify command `wc -l` inspects output with `wc` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command for the import-isolation AC, e.g. `python -c "import ast, sys; tree=ast.parse(open('src/rebar/llm/plan_review/coach_moves.py').read()); imports=[n.module for n in ast.walk(tree) if isinstance(n,(ast.Import,ast.ImportFrom))]; assert all(i is None or i.startswith('rebar.llm.review_kernel') for i in imports), imports"` or an equivalent grep/ruff rule.
evidence: No grep, ruff, or import-linter command is listed to enforce the constraint
The five named pytest suites test behavioral correctness, not import topology
findings.finding: AC #1 requires coach_moves.py to import 'only rebar.llm.review_kernel', but no proving command is specified to mechanically verify this import-isolation constraint — a stray additional import would silently pass all named test suites.
scenarios: A developer accidentally imports a passes.py symbol in coach_moves.py; all existing tests pass; the cycle guard is never triggered; the AC is declared done incorrectly.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC #1: `python -c "import ast; src=open('src/rebar/llm/plan_review/coach_moves.py').read(); bad=[getattr(n,'module','') for n in ast.walk(ast.parse(src)) if isinstance(n,(ast.ImportFrom,ast.Import)) and not getattr(n,'module','').startswith('rebar.llm.review_kernel')]; assert not bad, bad"` passes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the 'imports ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel' claim: coach_moves.py will also need `import json` and `from pathlib import Path` because the moved `load_move_registry` uses both — update the plan to list the full import surface of the new module.
evidence: passes.py:826: path = Path(repo_root) / '.rebar' / 'plan_review_moves.json'
passes.py:828: extra = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding='utf-8'))
passes.py:36: import json
passes.py:39: from pathlib import Path
Plan: 'Now-unused import json and from pathlib import Path are removed from passes.py' — correctly identifies they move out, but the destination description omits them
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new coach_moves.py leaf will import 'ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel', but the moved load_move_registry function uses json.loads (passes.py:828) and Path(repo_root) (passes.py:826), so coach_moves.py will also need 'import json' and 'from pathlib import Path' — the 'only' claim is false.
findings.location: Plan body: 'The new leaf imports ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel (for validate_move_registry + DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD)'
scenarios: If an implementer takes 'ONLY rebar.llm.review_kernel' literally, coach_moves.py is written without json/Path, causing a NameError at runtime whenever load_move_registry is called with a non-None repo_root.

If the implementer does add json/Path to coach_moves.py (the correct action), the 'ONLY' claim in the plan is silently wrong — a reviewer auditing the PR against the plan spec would flag the extra imports as a deviation.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the import-surface sentence to: 'The new leaf imports `rebar.llm.review_kernel` (for `validate_move_registry` + `DEFAULT_BLOCK_THRESHOLD`), `json`, and `pathlib.Path` — the last two are needed by `load_move_registry`'s `.rebar/plan_review_moves.json` extension path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC (or expand AC bullet 2) verifying that `passes.py` no longer contains `import json` or `from pathlib import Path` after the refactor.
evidence: Plan body: 'Now-unused `import json` and `from pathlib import Path` are removed from passes.py.'
ABSENCE: no AC bullet says 'passes.py does not import json or pathlib.Path' or equivalent direct verification.
AC bullet 6 (`make lint` passes) only implicitly covers this if ruff F401 is enabled for those specific imports.
findings.finding: The plan body describes removing now-unused `import json` and `from pathlib import Path` from passes.py as a deliverable, but no acceptance criterion explicitly verifies this removal — it is only implicitly covered by `make lint` if ruff's unused-import rule fires.
findings.location: BACK-COMPAT paragraph (last sentence) vs. Acceptance criteria bullets
scenarios: An implementer who forgets the import removal passes all explicit ACs (LOC check, pytest, lint if ruff F401 is suppressed) yet leaves a stale import — no criterion catches it explicitly.
If ruff's unused-import rule is not configured for this file, `make lint` will not catch it.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC bullet 2: '…and the now-unused `import json` and `from pathlib import Path` statements are absent from passes.py (verified by `grep -n "^import json\|^from pathlib import Path" src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` returning no output).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that 'Pure/mechanical' means 'no logic changes, pure symbol relocation along an existing logical seam (the Pass-4 coach boundary)' — not an arbitrary/seam-free split — to align with the parent epic's 'never mechanically' constraint (716f Phase 1 Decomposition).
evidence: Leaf plan intro: 'Pure/mechanical module-size split so `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` … graduates off the size gate.'

Parent (716f) Decomposition: 'Phase 1 — one child story per grandfathered module (12), each: split on a logical seam (never mechanically; never creating a <100-LOC file), landing each split as its own Gerrit change on main.'

The parent's 'never mechanically' prohibits arbitrary line-count splits that don't follow a call-graph seam; the leaf DOES split along a real seam (Pass-4 coach/advisory-triage boundary) but the 'Pure/mechanical' label obscures this.
findings.finding: The leaf self-describes as a 'Pure/mechanical module-size split' — conflicting with the parent's explicit 'never mechanically' constraint in Phase 1 Decomposition, which could be read as the leaf contradicting the parent's seam-quality requirement.
findings.location: Plan intro paragraph + DEVIATION section; parent 716f-b917-9fc8-41f5 Decomposition: 'split on a logical seam (never mechanically; never creating a <100-LOC file)'
scenarios: A reviewer applying the parent's 'never mechanically' constraint literally could conclude the leaf is out-of-scope for Phase 1, triggering a G7 block.
If a future reviewer re-reads this ticket as precedent for 'mechanical splits are acceptable,' the parent's seam-quality constraint gets eroded.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Pure/mechanical module-size split' in the plan intro with 'Pure symbol-relocation refactor along the existing Pass-4 coach/advisory-triage seam — no logic changes' to signal clearly that the split IS on a logical seam, consistent with the parent's 'never mechanically' requirement.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC3: since passes.py lands ~775 LOC (under the 800 cap), it graduates off the allowlist entirely and its docs/architecture.md remedy row should be **deleted** (not replaced) — confirm this is the intent, or add the replacement row text if a record of coach_moves.py's contents is desired.
evidence: Plan body: 'This ticket updates/deletes that stale row'
AC3: 'its remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md offenders table'

Existing row (docs/architecture.md:271): 'Extract the coach cluster (`MOVE_REGISTRY`, `load_move_registry`, `pass4_coach`) into a sibling `coach_moves.py`' — names pass4_coach, which the plan explicitly keeps in passes.py
Plan body: 'pass4_coach STAYS in passes.py' — a documented deviation from the architecture.md row's content
No AC or plan section specifies what replacement row text (if any) should document the actual post-refactor state of passes.py and coach_moves.py in architecture.md
findings.finding: AC3 says the passes.py remedy row in docs/architecture.md is 'deleted', but the existing row (line 271) names `pass4_coach` as part of the move target — a specification this plan explicitly deviates from. The plan body says it will 'update/delete' the row, yet AC3 uses only 'deleted'. No replacement row text is specified, leaving the executor with no verifiable oracle for whether a new row documenting the actual outcome (coach_moves.py gets MOVE_REGISTRY/load_move_registry/APPLY_NOW_MARGIN/triage_advisories; pass4_coach stays) should be written or the row simply deleted.
findings.location: AC3 / docs/architecture.md offenders table row for llm/plan_review/passes.py (line 271)
scenarios: Executor deletes the row without replacement, leaving architecture.md with no record that passes.py still exceeds 800 LOC (it lands at ~775, which is under cap — so deletion is actually correct). But the executor cannot be sure whether 'deleted' means 'delete entirely' or 'replace with accurate content'.
A future developer sees no architecture.md documentation for coach_moves.py's contents or for why pass4_coach wasn't moved, having to reverse-engineer the decision from git history.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'its remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md offenders table' with 'its remedy row deleted from docs/architecture.md offenders table (passes.py graduates under cap; no replacement row needed since coach_moves.py is a new under-cap module requiring no allowlist entry)'.
